evidence: libc/src/string/string.c:38-47 in PR #3 had `strncpy(dest, src, n); dest[n] = '\0'` which overwrites n bytes past where strncpy stopped, causing a buffer overflow. The fix was an explicit loop.
fact: When implementing strncat, never use `strncpy(dest, src, n); dest[n] = '\0'` — strncpy advances dest by the number of bytes written (up to n), so `dest[n]` writes far past the buffer. Use an explicit loop instead: copy up to n chars (stopping at src's null), then write the terminating null at the current dest position.

evidence: libc/src/stdlib.c: UToAImpl writes digits in reverse, then strrev
fact: kprintf uses a global Buffer[33] as a shared conversion buffer. This is exactly sufficient for 32-bit base-2 (32 digits + null terminator = 33 bytes). Adding a %b (binary) format specifier would work but leave zero headroom; any future change to UToAImpl that writes more than 32 digits would overflow.

evidence: flush_lgdt() does ljmp $0x08 which reloads CS with selector 0x08, setting the accessed bit on entry 1
Test uses exact equality: NEKOS_EXPECT_GDT_ENTRY(1, KernelCodeEntry) which fails if accessed bit differs
fact: When testing GDT entries via sgdt in kernel tests, the CPU may set the accessed bit (bit 0 of the access byte) on segments that were loaded via far jump. Tests using exact equality comparison (NEKOS_EXPECT_GDT_ENTRY) can fail unpredictably across QEMU versions. Use masked comparison or the NEKOS_EXPECT_GDT_ENTRY_ACCESSED pattern instead.

Kernel QEMU test output is captured from the serial port. The test framework prints test results via serial: test output begins with 'NEKOS TEST BEGIN', each failure prints 'NEKOS TEST FAIL', and the summary prints 'NEKOS ALL TESTS PASSED' or 'NEKOS TESTS FAILED'. The script `scripts/qemu-kernel-test.sh` boots the ISO in QEMU, captures serial output, and checks for the pass sentinel. `just test` runs this script for Debug/RelWithDebInfo builds and also runs CTest for userspace tests.
review_uses: Understanding how to run and interpret kernel test results in CI or local QEMU runs.
Debugging test failures that only manifest during kernel boot (not in userspace).
